audit_status.source_base: Hebrew 1 Samuel / Shemuel Aleph chapter 26, draft aligned later
audit_status.review_note: First-pass draft reviewed for Ziphite intelligence, the three-thousand-man pursuit, night entry, Abishai's proposed killing, anointed-language restraint, stolen spear and water jar, Abner's failure, David's exile complaint, Saul's confession, and continued separation.

verses.restored: Ziphites came to Saul at Gibeah, saying, Is David not hiding on hill of Hachilah, before Jeshimon?
Saul arose and went down to wilderness of Ziph, and with him three thousand chosen men of Israel, to seek David in wilderness of Ziph.
Saul camped on hill of Hachilah, which is before Jeshimon, beside road. David was staying in wilderness, and he saw that Saul had come after him into wilderness.
David sent scouts and knew that Saul had surely come.
David arose and came to place where Saul had camped. David saw place where Saul lay, and Abner son of Ner, commander of his army. Saul lay within encampment, and people camped around him.
David answered and said to Ahimelech the Hittite and to Abishai son of Zeruiah, brother of Joab, saying, Who will go down with me to Saul, to camp? Abishai said, I will go down with you.
David and Abishai came to people by night, and look, Saul lay sleeping within encampment, his spear pressed into ground at his head, and Abner and people lay around him.
Abishai said to David, Divine has shut your enemy into your hand this day. Now please let me strike him with spear into earth one time, and I will not repeat.
David said to Abishai, Do not destroy him, because who has stretched out his hand against anointed of YHWH and been guiltless?
David said, As YHWH lives, surely YHWH will strike him, or his day will come and he will die, or he will go down into battle and be swept away.
Far be it from me by YHWH to stretch out my hand against anointed of YHWH. Now please take spear that is at his head and jar of water, and let us go.
David took spear and jar of water from Saul's head, and they went. No one saw, no one knew, and no one awoke, because all were sleeping, for a deep sleep from YHWH had fallen upon them.
David crossed over to other side and stood on top of hill far away; the distance between them was great.
David called to people and to Abner son of Ner, saying, Will you not answer, Abner? Abner answered, Who are you who calls to king?
David said to Abner, Are you not a man? Who is like you in Israel? Why then did you not guard your lord the king? For one of people came to destroy king your lord.

This thing you have done is not good. As YHWH lives, you are sons of death, because you did not guard your lord, anointed of YHWH. Now see where spear of king is and jar of water that was at his head.
Saul recognized David's voice and said, Is this your voice, my son David? David said, It is my voice, my lord the king.
He said, Why is this: my lord pursues after his servant? For what have I done, and what evil is in my hand?

Now please let my lord the king hear words of his servant. If YHWH has incited you against me, let YHWH smell/accept an offering. But if children of humanity, cursed are they before YHWH, because they have driven me out this day from joining inheritance of YHWH, saying, Go, serve other gods.
Now do not let my blood fall to earth away from presence of YHWH, because king of Israel has gone out to seek one flea, as one pursues partridge in mountains.

Saul said, I have sinned. Return, my son David, because I will not do evil to you again, because my life was precious in your eyes this day. Look, I have acted foolishly and have wandered exceedingly.
David answered and said, Look, spear of king. Let one of young men cross over and take it.
YHWH will return to each his righteousness and faithfulness, because YHWH gave you into my hand this day, and I was not willing to stretch out my hand against anointed of YHWH.
Look, as your life was made great this day in my eyes, so may my life be made great in eyes of YHWH, and may YHWH deliver me from all distress.
Saul said to David, Blessed are you, my son David. You will surely do, and you will surely prevail. David went on his way, and Saul returned to his place.

translation_notes: This chapter repeats the sparing motif with significant differences from chapter 24. Both accounts require source and compositional audit rather than forced harmonization.
Abishai's interpretation of access as Divine authorization shows why opportunity, ease, or a sleeping opponent cannot by themselves establish moral permission.
'Anointed of YHWH' restrains assassination in this scene. It may not be used to obstruct investigation, removal from office, survivor safety, legal accountability, or resistance to abuse.
David takes the spear and water as evidence but maintains distance before confrontation. Nonviolent restraint does not require unsafe proximity.
Saul's confession and promise occur after previous reversals. Apology, tears, blessing, and familial language do not prove sustained behavioral change.
David's complaint that he has been driven from YHWH's inheritance describes political exile, social exclusion, and religious dispossession; it may not support territorial supremacy.
David and Saul go separate ways. The narrative validates mercy without compulsory reconciliation.
safety_note: Do not use this chapter to demand that threatened people enter a perpetrator's sleeping space, prove courage through exposure, or forgive without boundaries. Do not turn 'anointed' language into leader immunity. David refuses assassination, gathers evidence, speaks from a safe distance, and leaves separately.
